methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Qedma is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Asif Sinay, Netanel Lindner, and Dorit Aharonov that has raised $26M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The valuation analysis applies one reliable method due to data constraints. The stage-progression approach, using the $26M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage progression, implies a post-money valuation of $52M. The peer-median approach was not applicable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making a median calculation unreliable. Benchmarking to the broader sector median would introduce uncontrolled heterogeneity.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single method, supports a valuation range of $44M (low) to $60M (high), with $52M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the adequacy of the stage-progression multiplier for quantum computing specifically, absent sufficient narrow-set peer calibration, and the representativeness of total-raised-as-proxy in an early-stage, capital-intensive sector. Additional Series A or early-stage quantum peer data would strengthen confidence in this range.
